thread.title: 3 terrorists killed in K P engagements ISPR
thread.title_full: 3 terrorists killed in K P engagements ISPR

author: Our Correspondent
title: 3 terrorists killed in K P engagements ISPR
text:
Security forces on Saturday killed three terrorists while three others injured in two separate engagements in Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa, the military's media wing said in a statement.
"An intelligence-based operation was conducted by the security forces in general area Bara, Khyber District. During the conduct of operation, own troops effectively engaged the khwarij location, resultantly two Khwarij, Haqyar Afridi alias Khyberay and Gulla Jan, were sent to hell," said an Inter-Services Public Relations (ISPR).
In another incident, movement of a group of terrorists, who were trying to infiltrate through the Pakistan-Afghanistan border, was picked up by the security forces in South Waziristan District.
Own troops effectively engaged and thwarted their attempt to infiltrate. Resultantly, one kharji was sent to hell, while three khwarij got injured, the ISPR added.
"Pakistan has consistently been asking the interim Afghan government to ensure effective border management on their side of the border. Interim Afghan Government is expected to fulfil its obligations and deny the use of Afghan soil by Khwarij for perpetuating acts of terrorism against Pakistan," the statement added.
Security Forces of Pakistan are determined and remain committed to secure its border and eliminate the menace of terrorism from the country.
President, PM
President Asif Ali Zardari and Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif paid tribute to the security forces for conducting successful operations near the Pakistan-Afghanistan border in South Waziristan and Khyber district.
The president and the prime minister, in separate statements, lauded the security forces for killing two terrorists in Khyber, one in South Waziristan and arresting three others.
President Zardari expressed his resolve to thwart any attempt of the terrorists to disrupt peace and security in Pakistan, reaffirming the commitment to continue operations until elimination of the menace of terrorism.
PM Shehbaz said the entire nation stood with the brave soldiers of the security forces in war against terrorism.
